arts.ttl: Fiorentina hoping to keep up the momentum in Como
arts.sum: Fiorentina have won their last three matches against Como across Serie A and B, while they have never recorded more consecutive wins against them over the two competitions. Como have won three of their last five Serie A matches against Fiorentina (D1 L1), although their most recent such win was in December 1988 (3-2).

arts.ttl: Fiorentina’s Palladino: “Kean needed confidence. There’s magic between us”
arts.sum: After six consecutive victories, Raffaele Palladino’s Fiorentina seeks their seventh win against Como on Sunday. The Viola are second in the league table, just one point behind Napoli following a complicated start. However, the coach, as he stated to Sport Mediaset, never doubted his players: “Despite the loss against Atalanta, I realized I had a strong team that was growing. From there on, it’s been a journey of improvement: Bergamo was the turning point.” The turnaround also came for Moise Kean, who scored his first Serie A hat-trick in the last match against Verona and is now the second-highest scorer The post Fiorentina’s Palladino: “Kean needed confidence. There’s magic between us” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Fabregas: “Fiorentina are a very strong team, we only have 15 players available”
arts.sum: Como’s head coach Cesc Fabregas is gearing up for a challenging match against Fiorentina, acknowledging the strength of their opponents while also addressing his team’s injury concerns. Following a recent draw in Genoa, where Como managed to secure only one point, Fabregas is focused on the importance of the upcoming game and the need to capitalize on available points. “We will not have Mazzitelli available, who has had a problem with his calf and should return for the match against Monza,” Fabregas stated. arts.ttl: Serie A | Como v Fiorentina: Match Preview
arts.sum: Fiorentina are set to continue their impressive Serie A campaign as they face Como this Sunday, aiming to maintain their momentum after a successful run prior to the international break. Currently just one point behind league leaders Napoli, Fiorentina have been on an impressive streak, winning six consecutive league matches and remaining unbeaten in their last eight outings. Their latest triumph featured a stunning hat-trick from Moise Kean against Hellas Verona, underscoring his pivotal role in the team’s success. arts.ttl: Five goalscorers to watch out for this weekend: Marmoush, Gakpo and Kean to maintain fine form
arts.sum: Tom McGarry has picked out five goalscorers to follow across Europe's big leagues this weekend.
